title: Vulture
content:
{{CharHub|Vulture|Adrian Toomes}} {{LegacyNotice|1}} {{Character |image = Vulture.jpg |real name = Adrian Toomes{{Ref|SMH}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Vulture{{Ref|SMH}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |title = Chief |affiliation = [[Bestman Salvage]] (formerly)
[[Adrian Toomes' Crew]] |movie = {{Infobox List|''[[Spider-Man: Homecoming]]''
{{Collapse|''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |web series = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[The Daily Bugle/Promotional Campaign|The Daily Bugle]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |comic = ''[[Spider-Man: Far From Home Prelude]]'' |actor = [[Michael Keaton]] |status = In Custody}} {{Quote|And don't you ever, ever interfere with my [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|business]] again, because if you do, I'll kill you, and everyone that you love. I'll kill you dead. That's what I'll do to protect my family.|Adrian Toomes to [[Peter Parker]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} '''Adrian Toomes''' is the former owner of [[Bestman Salvage]] who lost his livelihood after [[Damage Control]], a joint venture between the federal government and [[Stark Industries]], took over salvaging operations in [[New York City]]. Recruiting his co-workers and equipping himself with a flying [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|exo-suit]] that [[Tinkerer]] created using [[Chitauri]] technology, Toomes took on the identity of the '''Vulture''' and became a black market arms dealer. However, after four years, his illegal [[Adrian Toomes' Weapons Trafficking Operation|activities]] were noticed by [[Spider-Man]], thus forcing him to fight the young hero. After sending [[Shocker]] to deal with Spider-Man, Vulture [[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane|hijacked]] [[Tony Stark]]'s [[Stark Cargo Plane|cargo plane]], although he was once again thwarted by Spider-Man. During their [[Duel at Coney Island|final clash]], Spider-Man defeated Vulture before saving his life and leaving him for the authorities to apprehend. Toomes was then incarcerated in prison, and in gratitude to Spider-Man for saving him, refused to give up the hero's true identity. ==Biography== {{MD|Adrian Toomes (Earth-199999)}} ===[[Bestman Salvage]]=== ====Raising his Family==== {{Quote|It ain't bad, is it?"
"No, yeah. [[Liz Allan|Kid]]'s got a future."
"Yeah, well... we'll see, I guess.|Adrian Toomes and [[Phineas Mason]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} Adrian Toomes had married [[Doris Toomes|Doris]] and together they had their daughter, [[Liz Allan|Elizabeth "Liz" Allan]]. In order to support his own family, Toomes had founded the salvage company, [[Bestman Salvage]], in [[New York City]]. Toomes discovered his daughter had a natural talent for art like he had in his childhood, which made him proud as he vowed to secure Liz's future.{{Ref|SMH}} ====Battle of New York Cleanup==== {{Quote|Come here, hey, lady, come on. Look I bought trucks for this job. I brought in a whole [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|new crew]]. These guys have a family. I have a family. I'm all in on this. I could lose my house."
"I'm sorry, sir. There's nothing I can do.|Adrian Toomes and [[Anne Marie Hoag]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} [[File:Adrian Toomes and Phineas Mason (2012).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes cleaning up the [[Battle of New York]]]] In [[2012]], in the wake of the [[Battle of New York]], the [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|Bestman Salvage]] had successfully gained the contract to clean up the city. Having shown [[Phineas Mason]] a picture that his [[Liz Allan|daughter]] had just drawn of the battle and the [[Avengers]] involved with it, Toomes oversaw all his men cleaning up {{WPS|Grand Central Station}}, telling [[Herman Schultz]] to use the [[Chitauri]] technology to take apart one of the crashed [[Chitauri Chariot]]s as their own tools did not work on them. [[File:AToomesMeetsDamageControl.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes witnesses [[Damage Control]]'s arrival]] Toomes confronted [[Jackson Brice]] for arriving later for their job, with Brice blaming his lateness on his alarm failing to go off while Toomes told him to stack the Chitauri armor plating as he had been asked to do. Toomes was then approached by [[Anne Marie Hoag]] and her team of agents, telling him that they thanked him for his efforts but that he was to withdraw his teams from the area as the [[Damage Control]] had secured the contract to clean up after the [[Avengers]]' future battles. [[File:AToomes-DamageControlThreat.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes desperately begs [[Anne Marie Hoag]]]] As Toomes explained that he had the contract to clean up [[New York City]], he concluded that they were now allowed to be there. Hoag was unrelenting, however, telling Toomes' Crew to stop all their work and then leave immediately. Desperate to keep the work, Toomes started begging Hoag to let him have the contract, as he had his own family to support and put too much money into this job already, but Hoag simply ignored his pleas and began to get to work. [[File:AToomesCrew-FiredFromCleanUp.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes learns that [[Tony Stark]] is to blame]] When [[Foster (Damage Control)|Foster]], one of Hoag's own men, mockingly told Toomes that he should not have overreached in his work, Toomes punched him in the face, causing all the guards around Hoag to immediately draw their guns and ordering that Toomes and his men leave the area immediately. Hoag told Toomes that if he wanted to, he could have still talked to [[Tony Stark]] about the contracts if he wished to continue fighting for the job to keep himself and all of his own men employed.{{Ref|SMH}} ====Turning Towards Crime==== [[File:SMH Vulture 1.png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes sees the report on [[Damage Control]]]] {{Quote|I'll tell you what, let's keep it. The world's changing. It's time [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|we]] change too.|Adrian Toomes to [[Randy Vale]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} Furious at the disappointment, Toomes watched the [[NY1]] report [[Damage Control]] being backed by [[Tony Stark]] to clean up the mess left behind after the [[Battle of New York]]. Toomes watched the report with [[Herman Schultz]] who then commented on how the system was now rigged against them all, with Schultz noting the [[Avengers]] were being paid to clean up their mess whilst they were left unemployed. [[File:SMH Trailer 55.png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes decides to keep the alien weapons]] As they watched these reports, [[Randy Vale]] discovered that the group still had a large amount of [[Chitauri]] technology left over which had not been handed back over to [[Anne Marie Hoag]] and her own team to be stored away, as was instructed. With [[Jackson Brice]] refusing to move it, Toomes had then decided to keep it, convincing their group to turn to a life of crime, by reverse-engineering and weaponizing these alien weapons for sale on the streets to keep themselves earning money. [[File:Vulture-FirstScene.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes creates the new [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|exo-suit]]]] Over the [[2016|next four years]], Toomes still worked alongside Schultz and Brice while having [[Phineas Mason]] use more advanced technology and creating an [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|exo-suit]] for Toomes, avoiding the attention of the authorities or the [[Avengers]], as they continued breaking the law. As Toomes flew into his base, he proudly told Mason and his crew that business was good as he delivered even more advanced technology for them to weaponize and then sell to all of their brand new buyers.{{Ref|SMH}} ===[[Adrian Toomes' Weapons Trafficking Operation|Weapons Trafficking]]=== ====Jackson Brice's Fatal Mistake==== [[File:AToomes-CuttingMetal.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes continues building all new weapons]] {{Quote|I told you not to fire them out in the open!"
"You said, move the [[Ultron Blaster Gun|merchandise]]."
"Under the radar. Under the radar! That's how we survive.|Adrian Toomes and [[Jackson Brice]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} A few months later, Toomes' arms-dealing businesses were still going strong, while Toomes himself had still continued being hands on and working in their [[Adrian Toomes' Headquarters|Headquarters]] cutting through pieces of metal to build new weapons, ready to be sold straight in the black market. However, he soon received a call from [[Herman Schultz]] that [[Spider-Man]] had come across their arms deal with [[Aaron Davis]] and was pursuing him through a neighborhood, as they tried to defend themselves. [[File:SMH Fan's Guide 28.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture ambushes and attacks [[Spider-Man]]]] Toomes had quickly donned the [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Vulture suit]] and flew off to Schultz's location where they were still trying to get away and were shooting at Spider-Man with the specially designed [[Chitauri Gun]], causing chaos across the streets. Upon arriving at his crew's speeding van, Vulture spotted Spider-Man about to catch up with their group. Swooping down, he seized Spider-Man in his talons, flying hundreds of feet into the air above [[New York]] much to Spider-Man's complete and utter horror. [[File:Vulture Eyes (SMH Trailer 3).png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture drops [[Spider-Man]] down into a lake]] However, as Vulture flew Spider-Man up, higher and higher, and further away from the truck, he prepared to kill the vigilante only for the [[Spider-Man Suit]] to deploy a parachute, ripping the young hero out of Toomes' grip. Seeing that his men had now made a successful getaway, Vulture chose not to continue pursuing Spider-Man and instead flew back towards his lair to regroup with the rest of his crew, leaving Spider-Man to fall into a lake hundreds of feet below. [[File:AToomesPostRescue-TinkererChat.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes turns down [[Tinkerer]]'s suggestions]] Vulture eventually successfully landed back in his base, enraged by what he had witnessed as he removed his own [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|exo-suit]] and waited for his men to return, furiously throwing his helmet across the room. As Toomes continued fuming, [[Tinkerer]] informed him that [[Doris Toomes]] had been trying to get in contact with him before noting he had finished designing the high vacuum seal for the Vulture Suit, only for Toomes to reject the design as he still did not want it. [[File:AToomesConfrontingBrice.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes being confronted by [[Jackson Brice]]]] Eventually, Schultz and [[Jackson Brice]] managed to get back into the base as Brice thrilled with the chase, ignoring the consequences of his actions. Toomes furiously confronted Brice, asking him how many times he had told Brice not to fire the weapons like the [[Ultron Blaster Gun]] in public, with Brice defending his actions saying that he had to sell the weapons to [[Aaron Davis]], despite Toomes furiously reminding him that he had told him to move them under the radar. [[File:JBrice-vs-AToomes.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes reminds [[Jackson Brice]] of the risks]] Toomes noted that Brice had risked bringing [[Damage Control]] to their doorstep if they were discovered, which could have even possibly led to the [[Avengers]] finding out about them and destroying everything that they had built. Toomes then began mocking Brice's attitude and recent claims to be the Shocker due to using a [[Shocker's Gauntlet|weaponized gauntlet]] taken from [[Crossbones]], which Brice just ignored, even still ignoring Toomes as he had noted how much he relied on this operation. [[File:SMH Trailer3 18.png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes inadvertently kills [[Jackson Brice]]]] Eventually, Toomes became fed up with Brice's recklessness and attitude, telling Brice that he was now fired from his organization. Now angry over losing his job, Brice threatened Toomes, saying that he would inform the authorities about their operation. Brice then asked Toomes what would happen if he had told [[Doris Toomes]] all about his business. This was a step too far for Toomes, who promptly picked up a weapon and shot Brice with it, instantly disintegrating him. [[File:AToomes-NowYoureTheShocker.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes names [[Herman Schultz]] "Shocker"]] Confused and shocked by these actions, he asked [[Tinkerer]] if he had picked up an [[Anti-Gravity Gun]], only to have picked up a [[Chitauri Gun]] instead. Unfazed by his sudden act of murder against his own former friend and co-worker, Toomes simply walked over to Brice's ashes as he picked up the [[Shocker's Gauntlet|Shocker Gauntlet]] and then passed it onto Schultz, sarcastically telling him that he had become the 'Shocker' now while everyone else looked on in utter horror at Toomes' violent actions.{{Ref|SMH}} ====[[Attack on the Damage Control Truck|Hijacking Damage Control Truck]]==== [[File:Vulture-MidTruckHeist.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture begins his stealthy weapons heist]] {{Quote|This [[Spider-Man|little bastard]] in [[Spider-Man Suit|red tights]] shows up, and he thinks he can tear down everything I've built. Really? I'm gonna kill him.|Vulture to [[Shocker]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} Several days later, [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|Toomes' Crew]] received the tip that a truck full of confiscated technology was heading onto the [[Damage Control Deep Storage Vault]] by the way of [[Maryland]]. With both the [[Shocker]] and the [[Tinkerer]] in the van nearby watching for outgoing distress signals, Vulture stealthily intercepted the truck. Anchoring his [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|wings]] to the truck, Vulture used a [[Matter Phase Shifter]] to enter the truck. [[File:Vulture steals from the damage control trucks.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture starts stealing from inside the truck]] Taking in the sight once he was inside without alerting the guards, Vulture started filling a duffel bag with all of the [[Chitauri]] and [[Ultron]] technology that he found. To his surprise, [[Spider-Man]], however, had shown up, causing Vulture to quickly exit from the truck with the technology that he had been able to take. Before Vulture could get away, Spider-Man used his [[Web-Shooters]] to snatch his duffel bag away from him before then choosing to insult Vulture. [[File:Spider-Man vs. Vulture (D.O.D.C. Truck).png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture fights against the [[Spider-Man]] again]] Determined to get away before Spider-Man could disrupt his plans again, Vulture regained the [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|exo-suit]] and then began furiously flying towards Spider-Man, trying and failing to rip the duffel bag away from his hands. Seeing that Spider-Man was about to fall back through the [[Matter Phase Shifter|Wormhole]] he created, Vulture shut off the Matter Phase Disrupter, sealing Spider-Man in the truck. Vulture quickly made his escape, angrily leaving all his stolen technology behind. [[File:AToomes-SpideyNewsReport.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes sees a news report on [[Spider-Man]]]] Back at the [[Adrian Toomes' Headquarters|base]], Toomes was fuming over losing the technology. Tinkerer offered to upgrade the Vulture Suit, so as to pull off an airborne heist against [[Avengers Tower]], but Toomes turned Mason down, asking if they had enough to sell to [[Mac Gargan]] which Mason confirmed. Toomes began muttering that it was all [[Spider-Man]]'s fault and he would kill him. Overhearing, [[Herman Schultz]] showed Toomes a news report on Spider-Man [[Rescue at the Washington Monument|saving]] Toomes' [[Liz Allan|daughter]].{{Ref|SMH}} ====[[Ambush on MV Spirit of America|Staten Island Ferry Incident]]==== [[File:Shocker & Vulture (Staten Island Ferry).png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes and [[Herman Schultz]] meet privately]] {{Quote|You're messing with things that you don't understand!|Vulture to [[Spider-Man]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} The next day, Toomes and [[Herman Schultz]] made their way onboard the {{WPS|Staten Island Ferry}} where they planned to have a secret meeting in order to sell more of their [[Chitauri]] and [[Ultron]] based technology to criminals. Once they had discussed their plan, Schultz left Toomes behind while he and [[Randy Vale]] dealt with their latest buyer, [[Mac Gargan]], while Toomes stayed behind and kept his distance, ready to suit up and get involved if anything was to go wrong during their dealing with Gargan. [[File:Adrian Toomes (Staten Island Ferry).png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes confronts [[Spider-Man]] on the ferry]] However, Toomes was soon informed by Schultz on the radio that the deal had been interrupted by [[Spider-Man]]. Upon hearing all this, Toomes quickly ran down to his van where his suit was being kept, seeing that [[Shocker]] had been subdued during the fight and his own [[Shocker's Gauntlet|Gauntlet]] had been trapped in the railing. Once Toomes knocked out one of Gargan's men to get to the van, he briefly locked eyes with Spider-Man before he then climbed inside of the van. [[File:SMH Trailer 49.png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes dons his [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|exo-suit]] and fights]] Just before Spider-Man could confront Toomes, the [[FBI]] showed up to arrest Gargan and Schultz, distracting Spider-Man long enough for Toomes to don his [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|suit]] and armed himself with a [[Chitauri Gun]]. Bursting through the van's doors, Vulture fired upon the agents and Spider-Man, who had managed to knock the FBI agent out of the way while Vulture inadvertently threw a car off the ferry which had collided with Gargan and knocked him into the water. [[File:SMH EW Vulture1.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture furiously fights against [[Spider-Man]]]] Freeing the Shocker from his webbing restraints, Vulture told him to get off the ferry as they were retreating from the mission. Unwilling to allow Vulture to escape, Spider-Man fired his [[Web-Shooters]] at him. However, Vulture [[Ambush on MV Spirit of America|fought back]] by shooting the Chitauri weapons at Spider-Man while cutting the webs with his wings. During their fight, Spider-Man gained the advantage by activating the Taser web when one of the strands managed to hit Vulture in mid-air. [[File:SMH Vulture x Spiderman 14.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture flees from the [[MV Spirit of America|MV ''Spirit of America'']]]] The shock of the Taser web caused Vulture to drop the gun, which reacted violently with the electricity, causing it to fire several lasers that cut the ferry in half, despite Spider-Man desperately attempting to web it up. Seeing his opening, Vulture flew to the top of the ferry, where the Shocker was waiting for him. With the Shocker aboard his wings, Vulture flew away from the battle before [[Iron Man]] could show up and assist Spider-Man with saving the ferry's passengers.{{Ref|SMH}} ====Convincing the Crew==== [[File:VultureTinkererShocker-DiscussingRunning.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes and [[Herman Schultz]] keep arguing]] {{Quote|So that's it. You just gonna run?"
"[[FBI|Feds]] were waiting for us. Now we're on [[Iron Man]]'s radar? Yeah, I'm running. You should too.|Vulture and [[Shocker]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} In the wake of their fiasco on the {{WPS|Staten Island Ferry}} during which they were almost all discovered while [[Mac Gargan]] ended up being arrested by the [[FBI]], Toomes was informed that [[Herman Schultz]] had finally decided to quit the [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|Crew]] and go on the run, citing that the FBI were waiting in the wings to arrest them and therefore the risk was to great, inviting Toomes to just come and follow him. [[File:AToomes-UpgradingSuit-SMH.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes decides to pull one more final heist]] However, Toomes noted that he could not simply walk away, as his [[Doris Toomes|wife]] and [[Liz Allan|daughter]] still relied on him. Seeking to convince Schultz to stay on, Toomes asked [[Phineas Mason]] to create their high-altitude vacuum seal for the [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|suit]] ready for their [[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane|Hijacking]] of the [[Stark Cargo Plane]] to steal all of the [[Stark Industries]] technology, and had asked Schultz to stick around for one last heist, which he reluctantly agreed to as the group got to work planning for the mission.{{Ref|SMH}} ====Learning Spider-Man's Identity==== [[File:Adrian Toomes meets Peter Parker.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes introduces himself to [[Peter Parker]]]] {{Quote|Nothing is more important than family. You [[Rescue at the Washington Monument|saved]] my [[Liz Allan|daughter]]'s life, and I could never forget something like that, so I'll give you one chance. You ready? You walk through those doors, and you forget any of this happened.|Adrian Toomes to [[Peter Parker]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} On the same night of the [[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane|heist]], the Toomes family also prepared for the upcoming homecoming dance at [[Midtown School of Science and Technology]], as Toomes personally warmly welcomed [[Peter Parker]] inside his [[Toomes Residence|home]] ready to take [[Liz Allan]] out to the dance and tried to make small talk with the uncomfortable boy, who was seemingly turning pale from nerves before the homecoming dance. [[File:Toomes talks to Peter.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes making small talk with [[Peter Parker]]]] Toomes then jokingly asked if Parker wanted any alcoholic drinks and noting that refusing was the correct answer. Toomes watched as a nerved Parker awkwardly took several pictures with Toomes' own daughter, with [[Doris Toomes]] questioning if her husband had gotten Parker's name right. Toomes then announced that he would then be driving the pair toward the dance, claiming he was going out of town with [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|Bestman Salvage]], promising this was the last job. [[File:AToomes-DrivingLToomesPParker.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes drives the teenagers to the dance]] During the drive to the dance, Toomes asked Parker about what his plans were post-graduation. Liz explained to her father that Parker had an internship with [[Tony Stark]]. Toomes' interest was piqued as Liz declared that Parker was even friends with [[Spider-Man]]. When Toomes asked what Spider-Man was like, Parker fumbled, prompting Toomes to ask if they had met before, stating that Parker's voice was familiar to him, as if he had recently heard it somewhere. [[File:Toomes suspects Peter is Spider-Man.png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes realizes Parker is the [[Spider-Man]]]] However, Liz carried on about how Parker came to their house party several days earlier and left after a few minutes and had mysteriously disappeared during the Decathlon. Realizing that his botched [[Chitauri]] [[Chase of Adrian Toomes' Crew|arms deal]] was on the same night as the party, Toomes began to suspect that Parker might be Spider-Man and inquired how Parker felt to have been [[Rescue at the Washington Monument|saved]] at the [[Washington Monument]], only for Liz to declare that Parker had not been with them all at the time. [[File:AToomesPParker-GunThreat.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes gives [[Peter Parker]] just one warning]] As Toomes' suspicions about Parker's true identity were confirmed, he still kept his observations quiet and inconspicuous. Once Liz exited the car upon arrival, Toomes told her to give him just a few minutes alone with Parker as he wished to give him the "dad talk". Once she left, he pulled a gun out from the glove compartment and confronted Parker, asking him whether Liz was aware of his duality and assuming by all Parker's silence that she did not know. [[File:SMH Trailer 43.png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes threatens [[Peter Parker]]'s loved ones]] As Parker listened closely, Toomes explained that he was sparing his life in gratitude for saving Liz's life while in [[Washington, D.C.]]. However, Toomes then warned Parker to stop pursuing him at his [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|crew]] or else he would kill him and [[May Parker|everybody]] he loved in order to protect his own family. Toomes then prompted Parker to thank him for saving his life, declaring that they were now even and sent him into the dance and show his daughter a good time before Toomes drove away.{{Ref|SMH}} ====Spider-Man Confrontation==== [[File:Adrian Toomes.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes anticipatively awaits [[Spider-Man]]]] {{Quote|Those people, Pete, those people up there, the rich and the powerful, they do whatever they want. Guys like us, like you and me, they don't care about us. We build their roads and we fight all their wars and everything, but they don't care about us. We have to pick up after 'em. We have to eat their table scraps. That’s how it is. I know you know what I'm talking about, Peter.|Vulture to [[Spider-Man]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} Fearing that [[Peter Parker]] would not listen to all his warnings, Toomes chose to send the [[Shocker]] to the [[Midtown School of Science and Technology|school]] to intercept him if he tried to follow. Despite Toomes' warnings, Parker did refuse to back down and, following a [[Ambush on Peter Parker|fight]] with Shocker, he soon tracked Toomes back to his [[Adrian Toomes' Headquarters|hideout]] with help from [[Ned Leeds]] and confronted him. Toomes tried to justify his actions to [[Spider-Man]], explaining that everything he had done was to support his family and claiming that the wealthy and powerful care nothing for the common people like them. [[File:AToomes-Smirk-SecretTrap.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes reveals his true plan to [[Spider-Man]]]] In an attempt to take Toomes straight into custody, Spider-Man used his [[Web-Shooters|Web-Shooter]] to trap him against the table. However, Toomes had continued to claim that [[Tony Stark]] had made his fortunes by selling weapons to [[Ten Rings (Organization)|terrorists]], just like Toomes had been doing. Toomes also noted that he could see why [[Liz Allan]] had liked Parker, noting that he had not been impressed when he had first laid eyes on him at the doorway of his [[Toomes Residence|home]] but had claimed that now he understood. [[File:AToomes-RadioYell.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes leaves the [[Spider-Man]] to suffocate]] When Spider-Man still refused to give up, Toomes revealed he had in fact been stalling and summoned his [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|exo-suit]] to fly into the room. When Spider-Man boasted that the exo-suit had failed to hit him once, Toomes noted that this had not been his intention. Spider-Man then realized too late that Toomes had made the suit break the support beams around his lair, trapping Spider-Man under the pile of rubble and leaving him to suffocate and eventually die in there. [[File:SMH Trailer2 47.png|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes puts on his upgraded Vulture Wings]] With Parker now seemingly being crushed to death as his hideout fell apart around him, Toomes stepped outside as he donned his Vulture Wings which had all been upgraded by the [[Tinkerer]]. Looking out just over [[New York City]], Vulture spied on [[Stark Tower]] and waited until the cargo plane filled up with the [[Stark Industries]] weapons and all of the other [[Chitauri]] and [[Ultron]] technology was moved out so he could then steal it and sell all of the technology on the black market.{{Ref|SMH}} ====[[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane]]==== [[File:Vulture1.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture waits for a signal to begin the heist]] {{Quote|Got a visual on the [[Stark Cargo Plane|plane]], but I'm feeling a little resistance."
"It's probably just drag on the new turbines. Look out for the cloaking cameras. Stay in the blind spots."
"Deploying high-altitude vacuum seal. This better work.|Vulture and [[Tinkerer]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} With all threats to his plans seemingly dealt with, Vulture sat over the ruins of his [[Adrian Toomes' Headquarters|Headquarters]] and waited for the go-ahead to begin the [[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane|Heist]]. Once the time was right, Vulture flew after and quickly caught up with the cargo plane leaving [[Stark Tower]] holding the equipment he planned to steal. Speaking to [[Tinkerer]] during his flight, Vulture noted that the [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Exo-Suit]] felt like it was dragging extra weight. [[File:Vulture-HighFlying.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture chases after [[Tony Stark]]'s plane]] Once he had caught up with the plane, Vulture managed to avoid being seen by the cloaking cameras and latched himself onto the side of the plane. Without causing any alarm, Vulture then attached himself onto the plane with the high-altitude vacuum seal before he cut inside using the [[Matter Phase Shifter]]. As he got himself inside, Vulture noted to Tinkerer that this had better work, as Tinkerer then reminded him that every box in the plane was worth a fortune. [[File:Vulture-HotDog.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes successfully enters the [[Stark Cargo Plane|Stark Plane]]]] Entering the plane, Toomes used the [[Anti-Gravity Gun]] to get inside of the cockpit, finding nobody in there before he then hacked into their systems to override their security with the help of the Tinkerer who was still watching down below while also then deploying the decoy drone to throw the plane's ground control, which [[Happy Hogan]] was monitoring, off his scent during their heist. With everything going to plan, Vulture removed his helmet and overlooked the plane. [[File:Vulture-StealingHelmet.jpeg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture steals the [[Stark Industries]] weapons]] Toomes discovered it was filled with even more than he could have hoped for, boxes of [[Chitauri]] weaponry, robotic parts of [[Ultron]] as well as various armors from the [[Iron Legion]], all of which would be worth millions on the black market. As Toomes looked at some of the [[Stark Industries]] technology which included boxes of [[Arc Reactor]]s which had been used by [[Tony Stark]], he then began seeking to find the best collection of technology to take away with him. Toomes then began going through all the boxes, throwing aside the one of [[Iron Man]]'s old helmets to get to the [[Chitauri Gun]]s which could all have been ungraded by the Tinkerer and sold on to make a considerable profit. [[File:AToomes-RedLights-PlaneHeist.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes realizes [[Spider-Man]] has returned]] While still marveling at the [[Avengers]]' equipment, Vulture discovered that the high-altitude vacuum seal had become dislodged, causing air to be sucked out of the plane and the alarms to go off. Charging to the security cameras, Toomes discovered that [[Spider-Man]] had survived their previous encounter, having broken out of the rubble and quietly followed him and was now attempting to knock his exo-suit off the plane, causing Toomes to curse in frustration. [[File:Vulture-FlyingIntoBattle-SMHC.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture charges into battle with [[Spider-Man]]]] Enraged that all his plans were once again being interrupted, Toomes quickly donned his exo-suit and [[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane|fought against]] Spider-Man outside the plane, using his claws on his suit to try and knock Spider-Man off the plane. Vulture attempted to quickly knock Spider-Man off, only to for him to use his [[Web-Shooters]] to stick to Vulture before they were both almost thrown inside of the plane's engine, with Vulture's wings taking some heavy damage as a direct result. [[File:Vulture-vs-SpiderMan-PlaneBattle.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture furiously trying to kill [[Spider-Man]]]] Quickly flying back to the battle, Vulture attempted to kill Spider-Man as he used [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|his wings]] to stab at the web-slinger, causing sparks and shards of the plane to be thrown around which destroyed one of the engines in the process causing the plane to lose control. Seeing what was happening as the plane fell from the sky, [[Tinkerer]] pleaded with Vulture to abandon the mission while they still could but Toomes refused to leave without at least one crate to make their fortune. [[File:VultureStealingBox.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture attempts to steal at least one crate]] While Spider-Man focused on attempting to use his [[Web-Shooters]] to redirect the craft away from [[New York City]], Vulture sliced the plane's outer shell with his wings to attempt to pick up a crate filled with the [[Arc Reactor]]s. But before Vulture could get a hold of the crate, the plane crash-landed on the beach at [[Coney Island]] and caused them both to hit the ground hard. Both survived the crash but Vulture's wings were heavily damaged as a result of the crash landing.{{Ref|SMH}} ====[[Duel at Coney Island]]==== [[File:SMH Trailer2 66.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture flying down to attack [[Spider-Man]]]] {{Quote|Time to go home, Pete."
"I'm trying to save you!|Vulture and [[Spider-Man]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} As [[Spider-Man]] slowly regained consciousness after the horrific crash landing, he took his mask off and looked around at the rubble of the destroyed [[Stark Cargo Plane]]. Because the crash had made his ears begin to ring loudly, Vulture used this to his advantage and was able to pounce at Spider-Man, who was caught off-guard, souring down from the smoke all around [[Coney Island]] and knocking Spider-Man over with force. [[File:PParkerInTheAir-VultureCatch.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture violently subdues [[Spider-Man]]]] Despite the damage done to his [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|wing suit]], Vulture sarcastically greeted Spider-Man and began to fly across the beach and smashed into Spider-Man, who was too disoriented from the crash to properly defend himself, allowing Vulture to pin him to the ground with his talons and punch him repeatedly in the face as before flying him into the air after Spider-Man caught a fourth punch and dropping him to the ground after Spider-Man broke free from his talons. Having already [[Duel at Coney Island|beaten him]] down into submission, Vulture then picked up Spider-Man with his own talons, and slammed him into the ground a few more times. [[File:SMH Trailer2 53.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture decides to spare [[Spider-Man]]'s life]] The pair had a brief conflict in mid-air after Spider-Man used his webs to latch onto Vulture and struck him on the helmet, which Vulture quickly won as Spider-Man was beaten into the ground. Having subdued his own enemy, Vulture then prepared to execute Spider-Man but became distracted when he spotted a crate full of [[Arc Reactor]]s. Feeling the heist was not a complete failure yet, Vulture dropped Spider-Man and went over to the burning plane and grabbed one of those crates with his pair of talons, attempting to escape and sell all of the [[Stark Industries]] technology on the black market for a massive profit. [[File:Radioactive Exo-Suit (Vulture - Coney Island).png|{{AltFloat}}|Vulture tries to steal all of the [[Arc Reactor]]s]] However, all of the radiation emitting from the arc reactors caused the [[Chitauri]] technology in [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|his exo-suit]] to short-circuit and with the suit's heavy damage from the plane crash had caused his wings to malfunction. Seeing the new danger as he had witnessed this happen in [[Washington, D.C.]], Spider-Man attempted to pull Vulture away from the Arc Reactors before the suit exploded, but he was too late and ignored his pleas to save him, overdriven by greed as he then desperately attempted to make his escape. [[File:VultureCaptured.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes is subdued and taken into custody]] Having ignored Spider-Man's attempts to help him, Vulture's suit eventually went up in a ball of flames. Just after Vulture had crashed landed, Spider-Man ran into all the debris and pulled him out alive. Powerless without his suit and weak from the explosion, Spider-Man was able to use his [[Web-Shooters]] to tie Vulture to a crate, where he was later found and arrested by [[Happy Hogan]] and the [[FBI]], with Vulture offering no resistance as he was taken into custody.{{Ref|SMH}} ====Incarcerated with Mac Gargan==== [[File:SMH Gargan and Toomes talking.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes is greeted in prison by [[Mac Gargan]]]] {{Quote|I've heard a rumor... you know who [[Spider-Man|he]] is."
"If I knew who he was, he'd already be dead.|[[Mac Gargan]] and Adrian Toomes|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} In the wake of the [[Duel at Coney Island]], Toomes was sent to prison impending his trial. Not wanting [[Liz Allan|Liz]] and [[Doris Toomes]] to watch that, he asked them to move away from [[New York City]]. In prison, Toomes encountered former potential buyer [[Mac Gargan]], who was heavily scarred following the [[Ambush on MV Spirit of America|Ambush on MV ''Spirit of America'']] in which Vulture had inadvertently thrown a car at him and knocked him into the river, where he was then arrested. [[File:IfIKnewWhoHeWas.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Toomes denies knowing [[Spider-Man]]'s name]] Gargan, however, insisted that he did not blame Toomes for his injuries but instead blamed [[Spider-Man]]. As Toomes listened, Gargan revealed that he had friends on the outside looking to kill Spider-Man and he had heard a rumor that Toomes knew who the hero really was. Believing that he still owed [[Peter Parker]], Toomes merely responded to Gargan that if he knew who Spider-Man was, he would already be dead. Toomes then left to talk with his wife and daughter.{{Ref|SMH}}{{Clear}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== *'''Vulture Exo-Suit Capabilities''': Toomes was outfitted with a flying mechanical suit created by [[Phineas Mason]] with salvaged [[Chitauri]] technology. As Vulture, he would use the suit for heists on other salvaged equipment from [[Damage Control]]. In appearance, it consists of a metallic body armor, outfitted with a winged steel harness. The wings contained propellers that would help him fly. He was also able to move the wing tips, which he used to cut [[Spider-Man]]'s web. **'''Superhuman Strength''': The [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Exo-Suit]] gives Vulture the strength to overpower both humans and superhumans, while the wings and talons are strong enough to destroy concrete and lift heavy objects with ease. He was able to do a lot of damage on [[Spider-Man]]. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Vulture's suit is fitted with metallic body armor, and is designed to withstand blows from superhumans like [[Spider-Man]]. Even by themselves, the wings are durable enough to blast through many support pillars of a building without breaking. **'''Superhuman Speed''': While flying, Vulture could reach great speeds. He easily caught up to a [[Stark Industries]] jet that was moving luggage to [[Avengers Compound|Upstate New York]]. **'''Superhuman Agility''': The suits jet propulsion systems allow Vulture to fly and maneuver in the air with incredible agility and coordination, despite its large and bulky frame. He spun around in the air in order to get [[Spider-Man]] off his tail, this was enough to break Spider-Man's enhanced grip. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': The [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|suit]] gifted Toomes with greater stamina, allowing him to fight [[Spider-Man]] for a long time and flying without tiring. **'''Flight''': [[File:SMH Vulture x Spiderman 7.png|thumb|250px|Vulture flying in the air]] Vulture's suit is outfitted with a powerful winged harness that also has jet propulsion systems which allow him to fly at high speeds and soar through the skies with ease. [[Vulture's Exo-Suit]] operates in a similar manner to [[Sam Wilson|Falcon]]'s [[EXO-7 Falcon|wing-suit]]. ===Abilities=== *'''Master Pilot''': Toomes easily piloted his [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Exo-Suit]] in order to help him with his black market business. Over the years of using the suit, he became a master at flying and maneuvering around obstacles. {{Quote|Business is good.|Vulture to [[Tinkerer]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} *'''Gifted Intellect''': Toomes is very intelligent, being able to deduce [[Spider-Man]]'s secret identity from existing information. He is able to see the profit in anything, seeing that the [[Chitauri]] technology could be used for more nefarious needs. *'''Expert Tactician''': Toomes was able to plan and execute the [[Attack on the Damage Control Truck|Attack]] on the [[Damage Control]] truck, and the [[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane|Hijacking]] of the [[Stark Cargo Plane]]. He even managed to stay off the [[Avengers]]' radar for years until he was caught by Spider-Man. His men follow and trust his orders enough to stay with him for four years after they started a life of crime. *'''Expert Businessperson''': Toomes is a very intelligent in business, being able to form his own secret business to sell inventions to criminals in the black market. He went many years without being caught. *'''Expert Thief''': After his crew ran out of [[Chitauri]] technology to sell and make weapons, Toomes [[Attack on the Damage Control Truck|robbed]] a [[Damage Control]] truck in order to get new technology to sell. Then, he [[Hijacking of the Stark Cargo Plane|hijacked]] a cargo plane taking [[Avengers]] technology to [[Avengers Compound]]. He was only caught because his wing suit exploded. *'''Combatant''': [[File:SMH Vulture x Spiderman 4.png|thumb|250px|Vulture fighting against [[Spider-Man]]]] Toomes is skilled in combat to a degree that he was able to best the enhanced [[Spider-Man]] multiple times during their encounter. *'''Expert Marksman''': Toomes was able to aim and fire a [[Chitauri Gun]] precise enough to cut through the webbing restraining [[Herman Schultz]] from a far distance. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''[[Vulture's Exo-Suit]]''': [[File:VultureFlyingWithHisCGun.jpg|thumb|250px|Vulture dons the [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Exo-Suit]] while on flight]] Created by [[Phineas Mason]], Vulture uses a suit made with [[Chitauri]] technology and outfitted with metallic body armor, outfitted with a pair of talons and a winged, detachable steel harness that allows him to fly. The wings consists of doubled sharp blades which are capable of destroying entire concrete pillars and can fold and cut through objects in a scissor-like fashion. The second version of Vulture's wings has a decoy drone stored in its back compartment and an add-on function which give it the ability to form a high altitude vacuum seal by bringing the wings close together. *'''[[Chitauri Staff]]''': A standard-issue weapon of the [[Chitauri]] troops that invaded [[Earth]] during the [[Battle of New York]]. Toomes used a Chitauri Staff to cut off the power source of a [[Chitauri Chariot]]. *'''[[Chitauri Gun]]''': [[File:Chitauri Guns (Vulture + Mark 42 - BTS).png|thumb|250px|Vulture stealing the supply of [[Chitauri Gun]]s]] A standard-issue weapon of the [[Chitauri]] troops that invaded [[Earth]] during the [[Battle of New York]]. Vulture uses their powerful beam gun to fire at [[Jackson Brice]], which disintegrated his entire well-being and is capable of bisecting the Staten Island Ferry. *'''[[Matter Phase Shifter]]''': Vulture used technology designed by the [[Tinkerer]] to be able to turn a portion of a surface intangible and transparent, making it look like glass with a purple glow. He would notably attach this device to [[Damage Control]] trucks and even the hull of an [[Avengers]] cargo plane to pass through them to steal the supplies inside. *'''[[Ferry Cutter]]''': ''To be added'' *'''{{WPS|SIG Sauer P226#P229|SIG Sauer P229}}''': Toomes owned one of these handguns and kept it inside his car. He used it to threaten [[Peter Parker]] when he took him and his daughter to their homecoming dance, having correctly guessed Parker's identity as [[Spider-Man]] and warning him to stay away from his business. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Arc Reactor]]s''': Toomes attempted to steal several Arc Reactors from the [[Stark Cargo Plane]] but his plans were interrupted by [[Spider-Man]]. He attempted to steal a crate full of them but the plane crashed due to his fight with Spider-Man foiling his plans. Toomes' damaged [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Exo-Suit]] had malfunctioned due to radiation from the arc reactors which allowed Spider-Man to subdue him. *'''Switchblade''': Toomes used this knife in order to free his hand from Spider-Man's [[Synthetic Webbing|webbing]] during their confrontation at his hideout. ==Facilities== {{Quote|Dude, a [[Adrian Toomes' Crew|gang]] with alien guns and a guy with [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|wings]]? Yeah, they have a lair.|[[Peter Parker]] to [[Ned Leeds]]|Spider-Man: Homecoming}} *'''[[Adrian Toomes' Headquarters]]''': [[File:Vulture Exo-Suit (SMH).png|thumb|250px|Toomes returns to his headquarters]] Toomes owned a large warehouse during his time working for [[Bestman Salvage]], however when his company was taken over by [[Damage Control]], Toomes had then converted the warehouse into his base of operations for his criminal career, housing all his illegally produced equipment while [[Tinkerer]] had then redesigned it. However Toomes was forced to destroy the facility when he was discovered by [[Spider-Man]], using his [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Exo-Suit]] to destroy the support beams and crush Spider-Man. *'''[[Toomes Residence]]''': Using the money he had earned through his criminal life as Vulture, Toomes and his family moved into a larger and more expensive home. While living at the house, Toomes welcomed [[Liz Allan]]'s date for Homecoming, [[Peter Parker]], before offering to drive them both to the dance. ===Prisons=== * '''Prison''': Toomes was sent to prison in the aftermath of the [[Duel at Coney Island]]. Toomes met [[Mac Gargan]] in the prison, who asked if he knew [[Spider-Man]]'s identity, to which Toomes replied he did not, before visiting his family. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Doris Toomes]] - Wife *[[Liz Allan]] - Daughter ===Allies=== *[[Adrian Toomes' Crew]] - Former Subordinates **[[Tinkerer|Phineas Mason/Tinkerer]] **[[Shocker|Herman Schultz/Shocker]] **[[Randy Vale]] *[[Criminal Avengers]] - Customers *[[Mac Gargan]] - Fellow Inmate {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Damage Control]] **[[Anne Marie Hoag]] **[[Foster (Damage Control)|Foster]] *[[Stark Industries]] **[[Iron Man|Tony Stark/Iron Man]] † **[[Happy Hogan]] *[[Spider-Man|Peter Parker/Spider-Man]] - Attempted Victim and Savior *[[Jackson Brice|Jackson Brice/Shocker]] † - Former Employee and Victim *[[FBI]] *[[New York City Police Department]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|SMH}} **{{Appearance|SMFFHP1}} **{{Appearance|SMFFHP2}} *{{Appearance|SMNWH}} (mentioned) ==Trivia== *In the comics, Vulture originally flew with a pair of magnetic wings to get revenge on his former business partner [[w:c:marvel:Gregory Bestman|Gregory Bestman]]. He was also one of the founding members of [[Spider-Man]]'s recurrent group of enemies, the [[w:c:marvel:Sinister Six|Sinister Six]], alongside [[Mysterio]], [[Doctor Octopus]], [[Sandman]], and [[Electro]]. *In the comics, '''Wilson Allan''' is the father of [[Liz Allan]] and the second husband of [[Doris Toomes|Doris Raxton]]. This is, however, not the first time Liz's father is a supervillain. In the Ultimate Comics, her father is the [[mutant]] villain [[Blob]]. *The collar of Toomes' aviator jacket is a nod to many of Vulture's costumes in the comics. *Vulture is the third superhero-type character based on a flying animal that [[Michael Keaton]] has portrayed, with the previous two being {{WPS|Batman}} in ''{{WPS|Batman (1989 film)|Batman}}'' and ''{{WPS|Batman Returns}}'' and Birdman in ''{{WPS|Birdman (film)|Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)}}''. *Toomes intensely dislikes [[Tony Stark]] for putting him out of business. [[Michael Keaton]]'s character in ''{{WPS|Birdman (film)|Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)}}'', Riggan Thompson, has a similar dislike of [[Robert Downey Jr.]] due to the fact that while they both achieved fame portraying superheroes, Downey is one of the biggest stars in Hollywood while he has faded into obscurity. *Toomes is not directly referred to as "Vulture" until ''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]''. In ''[[Spider-Man: Homecoming]]'', [[Iron Man]] said to [[Spider-Man]] ''"Look, forget the flying vulture guy, please,"'' which Parker later used to refer to him on the note he left for the authorities. ==Behind the Scenes== *Vulture in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] is the first live-action incarnation of the character. **In the original plans for an unproduced ''{{WPS|Spider-Man 4}}'', which was part of [[Sam Raimi]]'s ''Spider-Man'' film series, Vulture was set to be the main villain with [[John Malkovich]] cast in the role. The character had also been considered to appear in ''{{WPS|Spider-Man 3}}'' with [[Ben Kingsley]] being considered for the role. *In an early version of the character, Adrian Toomes was a member of the [[Air National Guard]] alongside [[Sam Wilson]], and the [[Vulture's Exo-Suit]] was a less refined version of the [[EXO-7 Falcon]].[https://www.instagram.com/p/BYR9vbmjaId/?taken-by=joshnizzi.art Josh Nizzi's Concept Artwork of Vulture on Instagram] The original pitch for ''[[Spider-Man: Homecoming]]'' by [[John Francis Daley]] and [[Jonathan Goldstein]] had Toomes as a teacher of the [[Midtown School of Science and Technology]] where he secretly crafted his wings, to connect Vulture with [[Peter Parker]]'s school life through Liz.[https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/pitch-page-screen-9-ways-spider-man-homecoming-changed-along-way-201352558.html From Pitch to Page to Screen: 9 Ways 'Spider-Man Homecoming' Changed Along the Way] *''{{WPS|Star Wars}}'' actor [[Mark Hamill]] originally wanted to play Vulture in ''Spider-Man: Homecoming'' only if Michael Keaton did not accept the part. Keaton initially turned down the offer, but later regretted it just in time and played the role. *[[Donald K. Overstreet]], [[Trampas Thompson]], and [[Marlow Warrington-Mattei]] were stunt doubles for [[Michael Keaton]] in the role of Vulture. *[[Donald K. Overstreet]] was a stand-in for [[Michael Keaton]] in the role of Vulture. *[https://images.thedirect.com/media/photos/IMG_0112.jpg Concept art] showed that Toomes would have appeared in ''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]'', which it would turn out he was released from jail and would be visited by [[Peter Parker]] asking for help with rehabilitating the captured villains. **[https://www.instagram.com/p/CmrsEGlvwqI/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=9e1b89a9-102d-45a6-accd-44627b24bf5f Another piece of concept art] showed Toomes with [[Sandman]] and [[Doctor Octopus]] in a warehouse surrounded by police, getting ready to face off against [[Norman Osborn]] after his inevitable betrayal. *This version of the character is shown entering {{WPS|Sony's Spider-Man Universe}} in the post-credits scene of ''{{WPS|Morbius (film)|Morbius}}'' due to a side effect of the spell cast by [[Doctor Strange]] in ''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]''.[https://twitter.com/CinemaBlend/status/1507437130963955725 Daniel Espinosa's Interview with CinemaBlend on Twitter - March 25, 2022 #1] In the post-credit scene, it is shown that Toomes made a new [[Vulture's Exo-Suit|Exo-Suit]],[https://twitter.com/CinemaBlend/status/1507437595931914243?s=20&t=Vej2NAl_zKxVcVFqEWE_7A Daniel Espinosa's Interview with CinemaBlend on Twitter - March 25, 2022 #2] and began recruiting allies for a new team.[https://twitter.com/CinemaBlend/status/1507438450668146692?s=20&t=Vej2NAl_zKxVcVFqEWE_7A Daniel Espinosa's Interview with CinemaBlend on Twitter - March 25, 2022 #3]As depicted in the film ''{{WPS|Morbius (film)|Morbius}}''. However, as of {{CURRENTYEAR}}, this has not been addressed by [[Marvel Studios]]. ==References== {{Gallery|Vulture/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{Marvel|Wilson Allan}} *{{WP|Vulture (Marvel Comics)|Vulture}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/vulture-adrian-toomes}} [[ru:Стервятник]] [[es:Adrian Toomes]] [[pl:Vulture]] [[fr:Adrian Toomes]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Homecoming Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Gray Hair]] [[Category:Gray Eyes]] [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Arms Dealers]] [[Category:Bestman Salvage Leaders]] [[Category:Adrian Toomes' Crew Members]]
